methodology: Two independent, complementary measurements of the same 7.2B fp16 shape-A (128in/1280out) decode step at bs=320 on the 5090, both reproduced in this session from landed raw artifacts (not re-quoted from memory).

scheduler_step_time.tool: bench-internal scratch/w1prime/steptime.py: parses the sglang server's own per-iteration 'gen throughput (token/s)' log line at #running-req==bs, reports the median implied step time (bs / thr_p50)
scheduler_step_time.note: distinct from the wall-clock sweep throughput in the wNprime_leg*.json files (client-side, includes ramp-up/ramp-down over the whole sweep); this is the server-internal steady-state per-iteration figure

rows.log: server logs not landed (verbose ServerArgs dump repeats every line; leak-scrubbing not worth it for a value already captured cleanly here) - reproduced 2026-07-13 from the tower's scratch/w1prime/server_legA_anchor.log

rows.leg: legFinal_A (state-fp16 + shift-lerp + addLN + GNGC + relu^2 + VRESGATE)
legG1_vres (same flags as legFinal_A, focused single-point re-measurement)

scheduler_step_time.headline: step_p50 39.27ms (anchor) -> 31.31ms (final, legG1_vres) at bs=320

kernel_time_breakdown.tool: scratch/gapattr/agg_trace.py against a torch-profiler chrome trace of our own runner (12 measured decode steps, bs=320, shape A, ANCHOR config i.e. before any W1' fix) - competitor-free: this trace and this breakdown involve only our own kernels, no competitor code or data
kernel_time_breakdown.trace_source: scratch/gapattr/prof_ours/*.trace.json.gz on the tower (not landed here: a 2.7MB gzipped chrome trace with no unique information beyond the aggregate below; reproducible from server_legA_anchor-equivalent boot + sglang's /start_profile)
kernel_time_breakdown.attribution_note: GEMM-family (~50-53% of GPU busy) already runs at the compute wall - not addressed by this work. Of the remaining ~16.8-17.7ms/step ('improvable', bucketing-dependent on whether the two small LoRA wmma kernels count as compute-wall or glue): WKV state kernel is 70-74%, the glue-kernel cluster is 26-30% - closely corroborates, without claiming pixel-exact reproduction of, the originally-cited 73%/30% split (that figure came from a session-specific bucketing not independently re-derivable byte-for-byte from this trace alone).
